claim: Despite layoffs, there is zero evidence of AI-related job losses at a macro level; job market remains strong with increasing employment
actionable: Do not fear job displacement due to AI; focus on learning how to use AI tools effectively to remain valuable
source_title: You get to keep your job

claim: Companies often use AI as a scapegoat for layoffs caused by overhiring during the zero-interest-rate era, not actual AI displacement
actionable: When a company attributes layoffs to AI, investigate whether the layoffs are truly driven by automation or by structural bloat
source_title: You get to keep your job

claim: Jevons' paradox applies to AI: cheaper technology increases demand for more work, not fewer jobs; productivity and employment grow together
actionable: Embrace AI as a tool to increase your productivity; as AI reduces the cost of knowledge work, demand for better work increases
source_title: You get to keep your job

claim: AI is middling: excellent at middle work (implementation) but still requires humans for prompting and verification at the ends
actionable: Your job will not disappear; instead, you will spend more time on planning (beginning) and review/verification (end) rather than implementation
source_title: You get to keep your job

claim: Companies are slow to adopt AI in transformative ways; most just use AI for simple Q&A and email summaries, not systematic automation
actionable: Be aware that moving from prototyping to production AI requires significant change management and organizational restructuring
source_title: You get to keep your job

claim: AI token costs are rising rapidly; Uber burned its entire 2026 AI budget in 4 months; models like Claude Opus cost $25/M tokens vs Sonnet at $15/M
actionable: Consider token costs carefully when choosing models; use cheaper workhorse models (Sonnet, DeepSeek) for most work; reserve frontier models for hard problems
source_title: You get to keep your job

claim: Anthropic and OpenAI are investing billions in consulting arms because companies do not know how to use their products effectively
actionable: Recognize that AI capability is not the bottleneck—implementation and organizational change management are the hard problems
source_title: You get to keep your job

claim: Most companies and individuals are bad at using AI; only hundreds of people in the world truly push the frontier of AI automation
actionable: Invest time in learning advanced AI techniques (multi-agent harnesses, RAG, evals) to stand out; simple prompt usage will not differentiate you
source_title: You get to keep your job
